plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-570 RP18.P06.M03.S09-RP18.P06.M04.S18 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-570 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP18.P06 implementation slice: the M03 Primary Implementation Slice tail (the twenty-two remaining rows) and the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice head (the first eighteen rows) as two descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H18), Claude review packet (C18), and CP00-571 handoff, without opening AI legal workflows, precedent, clause markup, or DD extraction runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-570 is a sound descriptor-only AI legal workflows P06 implementation slice (RP18.P06.M03.S09-RP18.P06.M04.S18, risk B, 40 units). Pack baseline is consistent across prompt, review-baseline.json, registry.js CP570 pack binding, contract current_pack, closeout-pack-plan.json, and manifest.json (deliverable distribution 15 implementation / 8 ui / 3 claude_review / 9 security_audit / 4 test / 1 hermes_evidence = 40; micro counts M03=22, M04=18). The pack-level production_ready claim is FALSE (implementation_layer=descriptor, runtime_ready=false, status=implemented_pending_review) and is well supported: it does not over-claim, does not open runtime, and does not weaken any production_ready gate. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced via freezeCp570Result (all runtime/write/leak flags false), a CP570 no-write attestation that correctly chains from CP551 (promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false), and ROW_EXTRAS containing only descriptor metadata (real_client_data_loaded=false). The CP570 validators, model tests, and contract validator deep-equality checks comprehensively cover coverage/section/authority-boundary/handoff drift, and command-evidence records all implementation+plan+repo-wide gates at exit 0. Claude is correctly bound as read-only independent reviewer and not final approver (authority_boundary + claude_packet + attestation), and Hermes is used only for deterministic descriptor evidence/receipt (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). CP178+ hardening is satisfied: forbidden_review_evidence covers auth-failure, zero-byte, malformed-JSON, fenced-JSON, tool-call-shaped, final-approval and enterprise-trust claims; readiness auth-preflight passed with non-empty valid-JSON stdout; no raw-output/receipt is prematurely present. One non-blocking P3 cosmetic note on review-harness scope labeling. Enterprise trust / final approval remains a human responsibility outside this receipt; this read-only review does not constitute final approval.

findings.title: Pack's own closeout-docs directory double-labeled in changed-file-scope (implementation vs unrelated)
findings.details: The path docs/closeout-packs/cp00-570/ (the pack's own manifest.json + command-evidence.json) is listed simultaneously under untracked_implementation_files (line 153) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 159). Labeling the same path both an 'implementation file' and 'unrelated' is contradictory on its face. This is review-harness scope metadata only; it does not affect CP00-570 deliverables, the active_pack_implementation_refs set, or the correctness of source inspection, and the directory was still inspected as part of the pack.
findings.recommendation: In the scope generator, treat the pack's own docs/closeout-packs/<pack>/ closeout artifacts as a distinct category (e.g. pack_closeout_docs) rather than emitting it under both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, so the categorization is unambiguous.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking informational note. Cosmetic tooling-metadata issue only; does not affect pack correctness, scope, or closeout. No action required for CP00-570 closeout; suggested for future scope-generator hygiene.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-570 ai legal workflows core p06 implementation slice descriptor pack
